rationale: In the citing paper, this reference is not a generic literature-list mention; it is explicitly used to support a specific recommended mechanism ('subverting categorical legacies'). The text directly invokes the cited paper's nested coupling concept and uses it again to justify when elite brands may be more open to such change. Although this appears in the discussion/managerial implications rather than in the core empirical model, it still provides a clear conceptual input to the citing paper's argument.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Dolbec et al. (2022)'s nested coupling idea to its own context, proposing that nonelite actors can gradually blend nonelite and elite heritage elements to shift what is treated as core. It transfers that mechanism from the cited marketization setting to the fashion status-mobility setting as a practical strategy.
